hooks.prompt:
Read the JSON on stdin. Check tool_input.command — if it does NOT contain 'git commit', stop immediately and do nothing.

Also stop immediately if the commit message (visible in tool_input.command or tool_response) starts with 'Update Playwright tests' — that is a test-only commit and should not trigger another test cycle.

Otherwise, do ALL of the following in order:

1. Run: cd "/Users/kelly.kenyon@glean.co/Documents/AI Testing" && git diff HEAD~1 --stat
   to see which files changed in the commit.

2. Run: cd "/Users/kelly.kenyon@glean.co/Documents/AI Testing" && git diff HEAD~1 -- index.html
   to understand what app behaviour changed (new selectors, new features, removed elements, etc.).

3. Read every file in tests/ that is relevant to the changed code. Decide whether any test needs updating — e.g. a new selector was added, a feature was removed, a label changed.

4. Edit those test files so they accurately reflect the new app behaviour. Do not delete tests unless the feature they cover was removed. Add new tests if a clearly-testable new feature was added.

5. Kill any stale server then run the full suite:
   pkill -f 'python3 -m http.server 3456' 2>/dev/null; sleep 1
   cd "/Users/kelly.kenyon@glean.co/Documents/AI Testing" && node_modules/.bin/playwright test --reporter=list

6. If tests fail, read the error output, fix the tests, and run again (repeat until all pass or you have diagnosed a genuine app bug).

7. If you edited any test files, commit them:
   cd "/Users/kelly.kenyon@glean.co/Documents/AI Testing" && git add tests/ playwright.config.js && git commit -m 'Update Playwright tests to reflect latest changes'
   then push: git push

8. Report a brief summary: how many tests pass, which (if any) tests you updated, and any app bugs found.
